You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository.
The codebase is at version 8.0 (commit: c4e4799f).

## Issue to Resolve

Folder assignments not correctly imported from K-9
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App version

8.0b2

### Where did you get the app from?

Google Play

### Android version

14

### Device model

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clear all app data to start from scratch.
2. Import settings from K-9.

### Expected behavior

All IMAP folders have to same assignments as in K-9. So, for each account the inbox, outbox, sent box, etc. are correctly assigned with their proper icon.

### Actual behavior

It appears that the automatic detection was used, because all folders were marked with "automatic" in Manage folders.

Also, various folders, including inboxes!, were not correctly detected and there is no option to assign the inbox in Manage folders.

### Logs

_No response_

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/Preferences.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

215→    @Synchronized
216→    private fun loadIdentities(accountUuid: String, storage: Storage): List<Identity> {
217→        val newIdentities = ArrayList<Identity>()
218→        var ident = 0
219→        var gotOne: Boolean
220→        do {
221→            gotOne = false
222→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", null)
223→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", null)
224→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$ident", false)
225→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature.$ident", null)
226→            val description = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", null)
227→            val replyTo = storage.getString("$accountUuid.replyTo.$ident", null)
228→            if (email != null) {
229→                val identity = Identity(
230→                    name = name,
231→                    email = email,
232→                    signatureUse = signatureUse,
233→                    signature = signature,
234→                    description = description,
235→                    replyTo = replyTo,
236→                )
237→                newIdentities.add(identity)
238→                gotOne = true
239→            }
240→            ident++
241→        } while (gotOne)
242→
243→        if (newIdentities.isEmpty()) {
244→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.name", null)
245→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.email", null)
246→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse", false)
247→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature", null)
248→            val identity = Identity(
249→                name = name,
250→                email = email,
251→                signatureUse = signatureUse,
252→                signature = signature,
253→                description = email,
254→            )
255→            newIdentities.add(identity)
256→        }
257→
258→        return newIdentities
259→    }

514→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
515→
516→        var identityIndex = 0
517→        var gotOne: Boolean
518→        do {
519→            gotOne = false
520→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$identityIndex", null)
521→            if (email != null) {
522→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$identityIndex")

524→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$identityIndex")

527→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.replyTo.$identityIndex")
528→                gotOne = true
529→            }
530→            identityIndex++
531→        } while (gotOne)

534→    fun move(editor: StorageEditor, account: Account, storage: Storage, newPosition: Int) {
535→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
536→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
537→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
538→
539→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
540→            .apply {
541→                removeAt(oldPosition)
542→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
543→            }
544→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
545→
546→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
547→    }

549→    private fun <T : Enum<T>> getEnumStringPref(storage: Storage, key: String, defaultEnum: T): T {
550→        val stringPref = storage.getString(key, null)
551→
552→        return if (stringPref == null) {
553→            defaultEnum
554→        } else {
555→            try {
556→                java.lang.Enum.valueOf<T>(defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass, stringPref)
557→            } catch (ex: IllegalArgumentException) {
558→                Timber.w(
559→                    ex,
560→                    "Unable to convert preference key [%s] value [%s] to enum of type %s",
561→                    key,
562→                    stringPref,
563→                    defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass,
564→                )
565→
566→                defaultEnum
567→            }
568→        }
569→    }

 12→class SettingsImporter internal constructor(
 13→    private val settingsFileParser: SettingsFileParser,
 14→    private val generalSettingsValidator: GeneralSettingsValidator,
 15→    private val accountSettingsValidator: AccountSettingsValidator,
 16→    private val generalSettingsUpgrader: GeneralSettingsUpgrader,
 17→    private val accountSettingsUpgrader: AccountSettingsUpgrader,
 18→    private val generalSettingsWriter: GeneralSettingsWriter,
 19→    private val accountSettingsWriter: AccountSettingsWriter,
 20→) {
 21→    /**
 22→     * Parses an import [InputStream] and returns information on whether it contains global settings and/or account
 23→     * settings. For all account configurations found, the name of the account along with the account UUID is returned.
 24→     *
 25→     * @param inputStream An `InputStream` to read the settings from.
 26→     *
 27→     * @return An [ImportContents] instance containing information about the contents of the settings file.
 28→     *
 29→     * @throws SettingsImportExportException In case of an error.
 30→     */
 31→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
 32→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 33→    fun getImportStreamContents(inputStream: InputStream): ImportContents {
 34→        try {
 35→            val imported = settingsFileParser.parseSettings(inputStream)
 36→
 37→            // If the stream contains global settings the "globalSettings" member will not be null
 38→            val globalSettings = (imported.globalSettings != null)
 39→
 40→            val accounts = imported.accounts.map { importedAccount ->
 41→                AccountDescription(
 42→                    name = getAccountDisplayName(importedAccount),
 43→                    uuid = importedAccount.uuid,
 44→                )
 45→            }
 46→
 47→            // TODO: throw exception if neither global settings nor account settings could be found
 48→            return ImportContents(globalSettings, accounts)
 49→        } catch (e: SettingsImportExportException) {
 50→            throw e
 51→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 52→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
 53→        }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    /**
 57→     * Reads an import [InputStream] and imports the global settings and/or account configurations specified by the
 58→     * arguments.
 59→     *
 60→     * @param inputStream The `InputStream` to read the settings from.
 61→     * @param globalSettings `true` if global settings should be imported from the file.
 62→     * @param accountUuids A list of UUIDs of the accounts that should be imported.
 63→     *
 64→     * @return An [ImportResults] instance containing information about errors and successfully imported accounts.
 65→     *
 66→     * @throws SettingsImportExportException In case of an error.
 67→     */
 68→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
 69→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 70→    fun importSettings(
 71→        inputStream: InputStream,
 72→        globalSettings: Boolean,
 73→        accountUuids: List<String>,
 74→    ): ImportResults {
 75→        try {
 76→            var globalSettingsImported = false
 77→            val importedAccounts = mutableListOf<AccountDescriptionPair>()
 78→            val erroneousAccounts = mutableListOf<AccountDescription>()
 79→
 80→            val allContents = settingsFileParser.parseSettings(inputStream)
 81→
 82→            val contents = filterSettings(allContents, globalSettings, accountUuids)
 83→
 84→            if (contents.globalSettings != null) {
 85→                globalSettingsImported = importGeneralSettings(contents.contentVersion, contents.globalSettings)
 86→            }
 87→
 88→            for (account in contents.accounts) {
 89→                try {
 90→                    val importResult = importAccount(contents.contentVersion, account)
 91→                    importedAccounts.add(importResult)
 92→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
 93→                    Timber.e(e, "Encountered invalid setting while importing account \"%s\"", account.name)

 97→                    Timber.e(e, "Exception while importing account \"%s\"", account.name)
 98→
 99→                    erroneousAccounts.add(AccountDescription(account.name!!, account.uuid))
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→            return ImportResults(globalSettingsImported, importedAccounts, erroneousAccounts)
104→        } catch (e: SettingsImportExportException) {
105→            throw e
106→        } catch (e: Exception) {
107→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
108→        }

111→    private fun filterSettings(
112→        contents: SettingsFile.Contents,
113→        importGeneralSettings: Boolean,
114→        importAccountUuids: List<String>,
115→    ): SettingsFile.Contents {
116→        if (importGeneralSettings && contents.globalSettings == null) {
117→            Timber.w("Was asked to import global settings but none found.")
118→        }
119→
120→        val accountUuids = contents.accounts.mapCollectionToSet { it.uuid }
121→        for (importAccountUuid in importAccountUuids) {
122→            if (importAccountUuid !in accountUuids) {
123→                Timber.w("Was asked to import account %s. But this account wasn't found.", importAccountUuid)
124→            }
125→        }
126→
127→        return contents.copy(
128→            globalSettings = contents.globalSettings.takeIf { importGeneralSettings },
129→            accounts = contents.accounts.filter { it.uuid in importAccountUuids },
130→        )

135→        return try {

137→
138→            val currentSettings = generalSettingsUpgrader.upgrade(contentVersion, validatedSettings)
139→
140→            generalSettingsWriter.write(currentSettings)
141→        } catch (e: Exception) {
142→            Timber.e(e, "Exception while importing general settings")
143→            false
144→        }
145→    }

147→    private fun importAccount(
148→        contentVersion: Int,
149→        account: SettingsFile.Account,
150→    ): AccountDescriptionPair {
151→        val validatedAccount = accountSettingsValidator.validate(contentVersion, account)
152→
153→        val currentAccount = accountSettingsUpgrader.upgrade(contentVersion, validatedAccount)
154→
155→        val accountMapping = accountSettingsWriter.write(currentAccount)
156→
157→        val incoming = currentAccount.incoming
158→        val incomingServerName = incoming.settings[HOST] as? String
159→        val incomingAuthenticationType = incoming.settings[AUTHENTICATION_TYPE] as String
160→        val incomingPassword = incoming.settings[PASSWORD] as? String
161→        val incomingPasswordNeeded =
162→            incomingAuthenticationType != "EXTERNAL" && incomingAuthenticationType != "XOAUTH2" &&
163→                incomingPassword.isNullOrEmpty()
164→
165→        var authorizationNeeded = incomingAuthenticationType == "XOAUTH2"
166→
167→        val outgoing = currentAccount.outgoing
168→        val outgoingServerName = outgoing.settings[HOST] as? String
169→        val outgoingAuthenticationType = outgoing.settings[AUTHENTICATION_TYPE] as String
170→        val outgoingUsername = outgoing.settings[USERNAME] as String
171→        val outgoingPassword = outgoing.settings[PASSWORD] as? String
172→        val outgoingPasswordNeeded =

175→
176→        authorizationNeeded = authorizationNeeded || outgoingAuthenticationType == "XOAUTH2"
177→

184→            incomingServerName!!,

1→package com.fsck.k9.preferences
  2→
  3→import android.content.Context
  4→import app.k9mail.legacy.account.Account
  5→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY
  6→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY
  7→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.OUTGOING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY
  8→import com.fsck.k9.Core
  9→import com.fsck.k9.Preferences
 10→import com.fsck.k9.ServerSettingsSerializer
 11→import com.fsck.k9.mailstore.SpecialLocalFoldersCreator
 12→import java.util.UUID
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.Clock
 14→
 15→internal class AccountSettingsWriter(
 16→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 17→    private val localFoldersCreator: SpecialLocalFoldersCreator,
 18→    private val clock: Clock,
 19→    serverSettingsSerializer: ServerSettingsSerializer,
 20→    private val context: Context,
 21→) {
 22→    private val identitySettingsWriter = IdentitySettingsWriter()
 23→    private val folderSettingsWriter = FolderSettingsWriter()
 24→    private val serverSettingsWriter = ServerSettingsWriter(serverSettingsSerializer)
 25→
 26→    fun write(account: ValidatedSettings.Account): Pair<AccountDescription, AccountDescription> {
 27→        val editor = preferences.createStorageEditor()
 28→
 29→        val originalAccountName = account.name!!
 30→        val originalAccountUuid = account.uuid
 31→        val originalAccount = AccountDescription(originalAccountName, originalAccountUuid)
 32→
 33→        val accountUuid = getUniqueAccountUuid(originalAccountUuid)
 34→        val accountName = getUniqueAccountName(originalAccountName)
 35→        val writtenAccount = AccountDescription(accountName, accountUuid)
 36→
 37→        editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.$ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY", accountName)
 38→
 39→        // Convert account settings to the string representation used in preference storage
 40→        val stringSettings = AccountSettingsDescriptions.convert(account.settings)
 41→
 42→        for ((accountKey, value) in stringSettings) {
 43→            editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.$accountKey", value)
 44→        }
 45→
 46→        val newAccountNumber = preferences.generateAccountNumber().toString()
 47→        editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.accountNumber", newAccountNumber)
 48→
 49→        // When deleting an account and then restoring it using settings import, the same account UUID will be used.
 50→        // To avoid reusing a previously existing notification channel ID, we need to make sure to use a unique value
 51→        // for `messagesNotificationChannelVersion`.
 52→        val messageNotificationChannelVersion = clock.now().epochSeconds.toString()
 53→        editor.putStringWithLogging(
 54→            key = "$accountUuid.messagesNotificationChannelVersion",
 55→            value = messageNotificationChannelVersion,
 56→        )
 57→
 58→        serverSettingsWriter.writeServerSettings(
 59→            editor,
 60→            key = "$accountUuid.$INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY",
 61→            server = account.incoming,
 62→        )
 63→        serverSettingsWriter.writeServerSettings(
 64→            editor,
 65→            key = "$accountUuid.$OUTGOING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY",
 66→            server = account.outgoing,
 67→        )
 68→
 69→        writeIdentities(editor, accountUuid, account.identities)
 70→        writeFolders(editor, accountUuid, account.folders)
 71→
 72→        updateAccountUuids(editor, accountUuid)
 73→
 74→        if (!editor.commit()) {
 75→            error("Failed to commit account settings")
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        // Reload accounts so the new account can be picked up by Preferences.getAccount()
 79→        preferences.loadAccounts()
 80→
 81→        val appAccount = preferences.getAccount(accountUuid) ?: error("Failed to load account: $accountUuid")
 82→        localFoldersCreator.createSpecialLocalFolders(appAccount)
 83→
 84→        Core.setServicesEnabled(context)
 85→
 86→        return originalAccount to writtenAccount
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    private fun updateAccountUuids(editor: StorageEditor, accountUuid: String) {
 90→        val oldAccountUuids = preferences.storage.getString("accountUuids", "")
 91→            .split(',')
 92→            .dropLastWhile { it.isEmpty() }
 93→        val newAccountUuids = oldAccountUuids + accountUuid
 94→
 95→        val newAccountUuidString = newAccountUuids.joinToString(separator = ",")
 96→        editor.putStringWithLogging("accountUuids", newAccountUuidString)
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun writeIdentities(
100→        editor: StorageEditor,
101→        accountUuid: String,
102→        identities: List<ValidatedSettings.Identity>,
103→    ) {
104→        for ((index, identity) in identities.withIndex()) {
105→            identitySettingsWriter.write(editor, accountUuid, index, identity)
106→        }
107→    }
108→
109→    private fun writeFolders(editor: StorageEditor, accountUuid: String, folders: List<ValidatedSettings.Folder>) {
110→        for (folder in folders) {
111→            folderSettingsWriter.write(editor, accountUuid, folder)
112→        }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun getUniqueAccountUuid(accountUuid: String): String {
116→        val existingAccount = preferences.getAccount(accountUuid)
117→        return if (existingAccount != null) {
118→            // An account with this UUID already exists. So generate a new UUID.
119→            UUID.randomUUID().toString()
120→        } else {
121→            accountUuid
122→        }
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun getUniqueAccountName(accountName: String): String {
126→        val accounts = preferences.getAccounts()
127→        if (!isAccountNameUsed(accountName, accounts)) {
128→            return accountName
129→        }
130→
131→        // Account name is already in use. So generate a new one by appending " (x)", where x is the first
132→        // number >= 1 that results in an unused account name.
133→        for (i in 1..accounts.size) {
134→            val newAccountName = "$accountName ($i)"
135→            if (!isAccountNameUsed(newAccountName, accounts)) {
136→                return newAccountName
137→            }
138→        }
139→
140→        error("Unexpected exit")
141→    }
142→
143→    private fun isAccountNameUsed(name: String?, accounts: List<Account>): Boolean {
144→        return accounts.any { it.displayName == name }
145→    }
146→}

1→package com.fsck.k9.preferences
  2→
  3→import android.content.ContentResolver
  4→import android.net.Uri
  5→import android.util.Xml
  6→import app.k9mail.legacy.account.Account
  7→import app.k9mail.legacy.mailstore.FolderRepository
  8→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY
  9→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY
 10→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY
 11→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.IDENTITY_NAME_KEY
 12→import com.fsck.k9.Preferences
 13→import com.fsck.k9.notification.NotificationSettingsUpdater
 14→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.ServerTypeConverter.fromServerSettingsType
 15→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.InvalidSettingValueException
 16→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.SettingsDescription
 17→import java.io.OutputStream
 18→import java.text.SimpleDateFormat
 19→import java.util.Calendar
 20→import java.util.Locale
 21→import org.xmlpull.v1.XmlSerializer
 22→import timber.log.Timber
 23→
 24→class SettingsExporter(
 25→    private val contentResolver: ContentResolver,
 26→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 27→    private val folderSettingsProvider: FolderSettingsProvider,
 28→    private val folderRepository: FolderRepository,
 29→    private val notificationSettingsUpdater: NotificationSettingsUpdater,
 30→) {
 31→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 32→    fun exportToUri(includeGlobals: Boolean, accountUuids: Set<String>, uri: Uri) {
 33→        try {
 34→            contentResolver.openOutputStream(uri, "wt")!!.use { outputStream ->
 35→                exportPreferences(outputStream, includeGlobals, accountUuids, includePasswords = false)
 36→            }
 37→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 38→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 43→    fun exportPreferences(
 44→        outputStream: OutputStream,
 45→        includeGlobals: Boolean,
 46→        accountUuids: Set<String>,
 47→        includePasswords: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 50→
 51→        try {
 52→            val serializer = Xml.newSerializer()
 53→            serializer.setOutput(outputStream, "UTF-8")
 54→
 55→            serializer.startDocument(null, true)
 56→
 57→            // Output with indentation
 58→            serializer.setFeature("http://xmlpull.org/v1/doc/features.html#indent-output", true)
 59→
 60→            serializer.startTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 61→            serializer.attribute(null, VERSION_ATTRIBUTE, Settings.VERSION.toString())
 62→            serializer.attribute(null, FILE_FORMAT_ATTRIBUTE, FILE_FORMAT_VERSION.toString())
 63→
 64→            Timber.i("Exporting preferences")
 65→
 66→            val storage = preferences.storage
 67→
 68→            val prefs: Map<String, Any> = storage.all.toSortedMap()
 69→            if (includeGlobals) {
 70→                serializer.startTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 71→                writeSettings(serializer, prefs)
 72→                serializer.endTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 76→            for (accountUuid in accountUuids) {
 77→                preferences.getAccount(accountUuid)?.let { account ->
 78→                    writeAccount(serializer, account, prefs, includePasswords)
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→            serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 82→
 83→            serializer.endTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 84→            serializer.endDocument()
 85→            serializer.flush()
 86→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 87→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e.localizedMessage, e)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids: Set<String>) {
 92→        try {
 93→            notificationSettingsUpdater.updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 94→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 95→            // An error here could mean we export notification settings that don't reflect the current configuration
 96→            // of the notification channels. But we prefer stale data over failing the export.
 97→            Timber.w(e, "Error while updating accounts with notification configuration from system")
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun writeSettings(serializer: XmlSerializer, prefs: Map<String, Any>) {
102→        for ((key, versions) in GeneralSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS) {
103→            val valueString = prefs[key] as String?
104→            val highestVersion = versions.lastKey()
105→            val setting = versions[highestVersion] ?: continue // Setting was removed
106→
107→            if (valueString != null) {
108→                try {
109→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting, valueString)
110→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
111→                    Timber.w(
112→                        "Global setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. This shouldn't happen!",
113→                        key,
114→                        valueString,
115→                    )
116→                }
117→            } else {
118→                Timber.d("Couldn't find key \"%s\" in preference storage. Using default value.", key)
119→                writeKeyAndDefaultValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting)
120→            }
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun writeAccount(
125→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
126→        account: Account,
127→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
128→        includePasswords: Boolean,
129→    ) {
130→        val identities = mutableSetOf<Int>()
131→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
132→
133→        serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
134→        serializer.attribute(null, UUID_ATTRIBUTE, accountUuid)
135→
136→        val name = prefs["$accountUuid.$ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY"] as String?
137→        if (name != null) {
138→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
139→            serializer.text(name)
140→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
141→        }
142→
143→        // Write incoming server settings
144→        val incoming = account.incomingServerSettings
145→        serializer.startTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
146→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(incoming.type))
147→
148→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, incoming.host)
149→        if (incoming.port != -1) {
150→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, incoming.port.toString())
151→        }
152→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, incoming.connectionSecurity.name)
153→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, incoming.authenticationType.name)
154→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, incoming.username)
155→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, incoming.clientCertificateAlias)
156→
157→        if (includePasswords && !incoming.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
158→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, incoming.password)
159→        }
160→
161→        var extras = incoming.extra
162→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
163→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
164→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
165→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
166→            }
167→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
168→        }
169→        serializer.endTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
170→
171→        // Write outgoing server settings
172→        val outgoing = account.outgoingServerSettings
173→        serializer.startTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
174→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(outgoing.type))
175→
176→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, outgoing.host)
177→        if (outgoing.port != -1) {
178→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, outgoing.port.toString())
179→        }
180→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, outgoing.connectionSecurity.name)
181→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, outgoing.authenticationType.name)
182→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, outgoing.username)
183→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, outgoing.clientCertificateAlias)
184→
185→        if (includePasswords && !outgoing.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
186→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, outgoing.password)
187→        }
188→
189→        extras = outgoing.extra
190→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
191→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
192→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
193→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
194→            }
195→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
196→        }
197→        serializer.endTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
198→
199→        // Write account settings
200→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
201→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
202→            val valueString = value.toString()
203→            val comps = key.split(".", limit = 2)
204→
205→            if (comps.size < 2) {
206→                // Skip global settings
207→                continue
208→            }
209→
210→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
211→            val keyPart = comps[1]
212→
213→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid) {
214→                // Setting doesn't belong to the account we're currently writing.
215→                continue
216→            }
217→
218→            val indexOfLastDot = keyPart.lastIndexOf(".")
219→            val hasThirdPart = indexOfLastDot != -1 && indexOfLastDot < keyPart.length - 1
220→            if (hasThirdPart) {
221→                val secondPart = keyPart.substring(0, indexOfLastDot)
222→                val thirdPart = keyPart.substring(indexOfLastDot + 1)
223→                if (secondPart == IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY) {
224→                    // This is an identity key. Save identity index for later...
225→                    thirdPart.toIntOrNull()?.let {
226→                        identities.add(it)
227→                    }
228→                    // ... but don't write it now.
229→                    continue
230→                }
231→
232→                if (FolderSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS.containsKey(thirdPart)) {
233→                    // This is a folder key. Ignore it.
234→                    continue
235→                }
236→            }
237→
238→            if (keyPart !in FOLDER_NAME_KEYS) {
239→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, keyPart, valueString, account)
240→            }
241→        }
242→
243→        writeFolderNameSettings(account, folderRepository, serializer)
244→
245→        serializer.endTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
246→
247→        if (identities.isNotEmpty()) {
248→            serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
249→
250→            // Sort identity indices (that's why we store them as Integers)
251→            val sortedIdentities = identities.sorted()
252→            for (identityIndex in sortedIdentities) {
253→                writeIdentity(serializer, accountUuid, identityIndex.toString(), prefs)
254→            }
255→            serializer.endTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
256→        }
257→
258→        val folders = folderSettingsProvider.getFolderSettings(account)
259→        if (folders.isNotEmpty()) {
260→            serializer.startTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
261→            for (folder in folders) {
262→                writeFolder(serializer, folder)
263→            }
264→            serializer.endTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
265→        }
266→
267→        serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
268→    }
269→
270→    private fun writeAccountSettingIfValid(
271→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
272→        keyPart: String,
273→        valueString: String,
274→        account: Account,
275→    ) {
276→        val versionedSetting = AccountSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[keyPart]
277→        if (versionedSetting != null) {
278→            val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
279→
280→            val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
281→            if (setting != null) {
282→                // Only export account settings that can be found in AccountSettings.SETTINGS
283→                try {
284→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, keyPart, setting, valueString)
285→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
286→                    Timber.w(
287→                        "Account setting \"%s\" (%s) has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +
288→                            "This shouldn't happen!",
289→                        keyPart,
290→                        account,
291→                        valueString,
292→                    )
293→                }
294→            }
295→        }
296→    }
297→
298→    private fun writeFolderNameSettings(
299→        account: Account,
300→        folderRepository: FolderRepository,
301→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
302→    ) {
303→        fun writeFolderNameSetting(
304→            key: String,
305→            folderId: Long?,
306→            importedFolderServerId: String?,
307→            writeEmptyValue: Boolean = false,
308→        ) {
309→            val folderServerId = folderId?.let {
310→                folderRepository.getFolderServerId(account, folderId)
311→            } ?: importedFolderServerId
312→
313→            if (folderServerId != null) {
314→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, folderServerId, account)
315→            } else if (writeEmptyValue) {
316→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, valueString = "", account)
317→            }
318→        }
319→
320→        writeFolderNameSetting(
321→            "autoExpandFolderName",
322→            account.autoExpandFolderId,
323→            account.importedAutoExpandFolder,
324→            writeEmptyValue = true,
325→        )
326→        writeFolderNameSetting("archiveFolderName", account.archiveFolderId, account.importedArchiveFolder)
327→        writeFolderNameSetting("draftsFolderName", account.draftsFolderId, account.importedDraftsFolder)
328→        writeFolderNameSetting("sentFolderName", account.sentFolderId, account.importedSentFolder)
329→        writeFolderNameSetting("spamFolderName", account.spamFolderId, account.importedSpamFolder)
330→        writeFolderNameSetting("trashFolderName", account.trashFolderId, account.importedTrashFolder)
331→    }
332→
333→    private fun writeIdentity(
334→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
335→        accountUuid: String,
336→        identity: String,
337→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
338→    ) {
339→        serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
340→
341→        val prefix = "$accountUuid."
342→        val suffix = ".$identity"
343→
344→        // Write name belonging to the identity
345→        val name = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_NAME_KEY + suffix] as String?
346→        if (name != null) {
347→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
348→            serializer.text(name)
349→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
350→        }
351→
352→        // Write email address belonging to the identity
353→        val email = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY + suffix] as String?
354→        serializer.startTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
355→        serializer.text(email)
356→        serializer.endTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
357→
358→        // Write identity description
359→        val description = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY + suffix] as String?
360→        if (description != null) {
361→            serializer.startTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
362→            serializer.text(description)
363→            serializer.endTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
364→        }
365→
366→        // Write identity settings
367→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
368→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
369→            val valueString = value.toString()
370→
371→            val comps = key.split(".")
372→            if (comps.size < 3) {
373→                // Skip non-identity config entries
374→                continue
375→            }
376→
377→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
378→            val identityKey = comps[1]
379→            val identityIndex = comps[2]
380→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid || identityIndex != identity) {
381→                // Skip entries that belong to another identity
382→                continue
383→            }
384→
385→            val versionedSetting = IdentitySettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[identityKey]
386→            if (versionedSetting != null) {
387→                val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
388→                val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
389→                if (setting != null) {
390→                    // Only write settings that have an entry in IdentitySettings.SETTINGS
391→                    try {
392→                        writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, identityKey, setting, valueString)
393→                    } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
394→                        Timber.w(
395→                            "Identity setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +

215→    @Synchronized
216→    private fun loadIdentities(accountUuid: String, storage: Storage): List<Identity> {
217→        val newIdentities = ArrayList<Identity>()
218→        var ident = 0
219→        var gotOne: Boolean
220→        do {
221→            gotOne = false
222→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", null)
223→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", null)
224→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$ident", false)
225→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature.$ident", null)
226→            val description = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", null)
227→            val replyTo = storage.getString("$accountUuid.replyTo.$ident", null)
228→            if (email != null) {
229→                val identity = Identity(
230→                    name = name,
231→                    email = email,
232→                    signatureUse = signatureUse,
233→                    signature = signature,
234→                    description = description,
235→                    replyTo = replyTo,
236→                )
237→                newIdentities.add(identity)
238→                gotOne = true
239→            }
240→            ident++
241→        } while (gotOne)
242→
243→        if (newIdentities.isEmpty()) {
244→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.name", null)
245→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.email", null)
246→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse", false)
247→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature", null)
248→            val identity = Identity(
249→                name = name,
250→                email = email,
251→                signatureUse = signatureUse,
252→                signature = signature,
253→                description = email,
254→            )
255→            newIdentities.add(identity)
256→        }
257→
258→        return newIdentities
259→    }

263→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
264→
265→        if (!storage.getString("accountUuids", "").contains(account.uuid)) {
266→            var accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "")
267→            accountUuids += (if (accountUuids.isNotEmpty()) "," else "") + account.uuid
268→            editor.putString("accountUuids", accountUuids)

271→        with(account) {
272→            editor.putString(
273→                "$accountUuid.$INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY",

276→            editor.putString(

534→    fun move(editor: StorageEditor, account: Account, storage: Storage, newPosition: Int) {
535→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
536→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
537→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
538→
539→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
540→            .apply {
541→                removeAt(oldPosition)
542→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
543→            }
544→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
545→
546→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
547→    }

550→        val stringPref = storage.getString(key, null)
551→
552→        return if (stringPref == null) {
553→            defaultEnum
554→        } else {
555→            try {
556→                java.lang.Enum.valueOf<T>(defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass, stringPref)
557→            } catch (ex: IllegalArgumentException) {
558→                Timber.w(

561→                    key,
562→                    stringPref,
563→                    defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass,
564→                )
565→
566→                defaultEnum
567→            }
568→        }

  3→import com.fsck.k9.helper.mapCollectionToSet
  4→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.ServerSettingsDescriptions.AUTHENTICATION_TYPE

 10→import timber.log.Timber

 12→class SettingsImporter internal constructor(
 13→    private val settingsFileParser: SettingsFileParser,
 14→    private val generalSettingsValidator: GeneralSettingsValidator,
 15→    private val accountSettingsValidator: AccountSettingsValidator,
 16→    private val generalSettingsUpgrader: GeneralSettingsUpgrader,
 17→    private val accountSettingsUpgrader: AccountSettingsUpgrader,
 18→    private val generalSettingsWriter: GeneralSettingsWriter,
 19→    private val accountSettingsWriter: AccountSettingsWriter,
 20→) {
 21→    /**
 22→     * Parses an import [InputStream] and returns information on whether it contains global settings and/or account
 23→     * settings. For all account configurations found, the name of the account along with the account UUID is returned.
 24→     *
 25→     * @param inputStream An `InputStream` to read the settings from.
 26→     *
 27→     * @return An [ImportContents] instance containing information about the contents of the settings file.
 28→     *
 29→     * @throws SettingsImportExportException In case of an error.
 30→     */
 31→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
 32→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 33→    fun getImportStreamContents(inputStream: InputStream): ImportContents {
 34→        try {
 35→            val imported = settingsFileParser.parseSettings(inputStream)
 36→
 37→            // If the stream contains global settings the "globalSettings" member will not be null
 38→            val globalSettings = (imported.globalSettings != null)
 39→
 40→            val accounts = imported.accounts.map { importedAccount ->
 41→                AccountDescription(
 42→                    name = getAccountDisplayName(importedAccount),
 43→                    uuid = importedAccount.uuid,
 44→                )
 45→            }
 46→
 47→            // TODO: throw exception if neither global settings nor account settings could be found
 48→            return ImportContents(globalSettings, accounts)
 49→        } catch (e: SettingsImportExportException) {
 50→            throw e
 51→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 52→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
 53→        }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    /**
 57→     * Reads an import [InputStream] and imports the global settings and/or account configurations specified by the
 58→     * arguments.
 59→     *
 60→     * @param inputStream The `InputStream` to read the settings from.
 61→     * @param globalSettings `true` if global settings should be imported from the file.
 62→     * @param accountUuids A list of UUIDs of the accounts that should be imported.
 63→     *
 64→     * @return An [ImportResults] instance containing information about errors and successfully imported accounts.
 65→     *
 66→     * @throws SettingsImportExportException In case of an error.
 67→     */
 68→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
 69→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 70→    fun importSettings(
 71→        inputStream: InputStream,
 72→        globalSettings: Boolean,
 73→        accountUuids: List<String>,
 74→    ): ImportResults {
 75→        try {
 76→            var globalSettingsImported = false
 77→            val importedAccounts = mutableListOf<AccountDescriptionPair>()
 78→            val erroneousAccounts = mutableListOf<AccountDescription>()
 79→
 80→            val allContents = settingsFileParser.parseSettings(inputStream)
 81→
 82→            val contents = filterSettings(allContents, globalSettings, accountUuids)
 83→
 84→            if (contents.globalSettings != null) {
 85→                globalSettingsImported = importGeneralSettings(contents.contentVersion, contents.globalSettings)
 86→            }
 87→
 88→            for (account in contents.accounts) {
 89→                try {
 90→                    val importResult = importAccount(contents.contentVersion, account)
 91→                    importedAccounts.add(importResult)
 92→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
 93→                    Timber.e(e, "Encountered invalid setting while importing account \"%s\"", account.name)
 94→
 95→                    erroneousAccounts.add(AccountDescription(account.name!!, account.uuid))
 96→                } catch (e: Exception) {
 97→                    Timber.e(e, "Exception while importing account \"%s\"", account.name)
 98→
 99→                    erroneousAccounts.add(AccountDescription(account.name!!, account.uuid))
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→            return ImportResults(globalSettingsImported, importedAccounts, erroneousAccounts)
104→        } catch (e: SettingsImportExportException) {
105→            throw e
106→        } catch (e: Exception) {
107→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
108→        }
109→    }
110→
111→    private fun filterSettings(
112→        contents: SettingsFile.Contents,
113→        importGeneralSettings: Boolean,
114→        importAccountUuids: List<String>,
115→    ): SettingsFile.Contents {
116→        if (importGeneralSettings && contents.globalSettings == null) {
117→            Timber.w("Was asked to import global settings but none found.")
118→        }
119→
120→        val accountUuids = contents.accounts.mapCollectionToSet { it.uuid }
121→        for (importAccountUuid in importAccountUuids) {
122→            if (importAccountUuid !in accountUuids) {
123→                Timber.w("Was asked to import account %s. But this account wasn't found.", importAccountUuid)
124→            }
125→        }
126→
127→        return contents.copy(
128→            globalSettings = contents.globalSettings.takeIf { importGeneralSettings },
129→            accounts = contents.accounts.filter { it.uuid in importAccountUuids },
130→        )
131→    }
132→
133→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
134→    private fun importGeneralSettings(contentVersion: Int, settings: SettingsMap): Boolean {
135→        return try {
136→            val validatedSettings = generalSettingsValidator.validate(contentVersion, settings)
137→
138→            val currentSettings = generalSettingsUpgrader.upgrade(contentVersion, validatedSettings)
139→
140→            generalSettingsWriter.write(currentSettings)
141→        } catch (e: Exception) {
142→            Timber.e(e, "Exception while importing general settings")
143→            false
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun importAccount(
148→        contentVersion: Int,
149→        account: SettingsFile.Account,
150→    ): AccountDescriptionPair {
151→        val validatedAccount = accountSettingsValidator.validate(contentVersion, account)
152→
153→        val currentAccount = accountSettingsUpgrader.upgrade(contentVersion, validatedAccount)
154→
155→        val accountMapping = accountSettingsWriter.write(currentAccount)
156→
157→        val incoming = currentAccount.incoming
158→        val incomingServerName = incoming.settings[HOST] as? String
159→        val incomingAuthenticationType = incoming.settings[AUTHENTICATION_TYPE] as String
160→        val incomingPassword = incoming.settings[PASSWORD] as? String
161→        val incomingPasswordNeeded =
162→            incomingAuthenticationType != "EXTERNAL" && incomingAuthenticationType != "XOAUTH2" &&
163→                incomingPassword.isNullOrEmpty()
164→
165→        var authorizationNeeded = incomingAuthenticationType == "XOAUTH2"
166→
167→        val outgoing = currentAccount.outgoing
168→        val outgoingServerName = outgoing.settings[HOST] as? String
169→        val outgoingAuthenticationType = outgoing.settings[AUTHENTICATION_TYPE] as String
170→        val outgoingUsername = outgoing.settings[USERNAME] as String
171→        val outgoingPassword = outgoing.settings[PASSWORD] as? String
172→        val outgoingPasswordNeeded =
173→            outgoingAuthenticationType != "EXTERNAL" && outgoingAuthenticationType != "XOAUTH2" &&
174→                outgoingUsername.isNotEmpty() && outgoingPassword.isNullOrEmpty()
175→
176→        authorizationNeeded = authorizationNeeded || outgoingAuthenticationType == "XOAUTH2"
177→
178→        return AccountDescriptionPair(
179→            accountMapping.first,
180→            accountMapping.second,
181→            authorizationNeeded,
182→            incomingPasswordNeeded,
183→            outgoingPasswordNeeded,
184→            incomingServerName!!,
185→            outgoingServerName!!,
186→        )
187→    }
188→
189→    private fun getAccountDisplayName(account: SettingsFile.Account): String {
190→        return account.name?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
191→            ?: account.identities?.firstOrNull()?.email
192→            ?: error("Account name missing")
193→    }
194→}

532→    @Suppress("MagicNumber")
533→    val earliestPollDate: Date?
534→        get() {
535→            val age = maximumPolledMessageAge.takeIf { it >= 0 } ?: return null
536→
537→            val now = Calendar.getInstance()
538→            now[Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY] = 0
539→            now[Calendar.MINUTE] = 0
540→            now[Calendar.SECOND] = 0
541→            now[Calendar.MILLISECOND] = 0
542→
543→            if (age < 28) {
544→                now.add(Calendar.DATE, age * -1)
545→            } else {
546→                when (age) {
547→                    28 -> now.add(Calendar.MONTH, -1)
548→                    56 -> now.add(Calendar.MONTH, -2)
549→                    84 -> now.add(Calendar.MONTH, -3)
550→                    168 -> now.add(Calendar.MONTH, -6)
551→                    365 -> now.add(Calendar.YEAR, -1)
552→                }
553→            }
554→
555→            return now.time
556→        }

  4→import app.k9mail.legacy.account.Account
  5→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY
  6→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY
  7→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.OUTGOING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY
  8→import com.fsck.k9.Core
  9→import com.fsck.k9.Preferences
 10→import com.fsck.k9.ServerSettingsSerializer
 11→import com.fsck.k9.mailstore.SpecialLocalFoldersCreator
 12→import java.util.UUID
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.Clock
 14→
 15→internal class AccountSettingsWriter(
 16→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 17→    private val localFoldersCreator: SpecialLocalFoldersCreator,
 18→    private val clock: Clock,
 19→    serverSettingsSerializer: ServerSettingsSerializer,
 20→    private val context: Context,
 21→) {
 22→    private val identitySettingsWriter = IdentitySettingsWriter()
 23→    private val folderSettingsWriter = FolderSettingsWriter()
 24→    private val serverSettingsWriter = ServerSettingsWriter(serverSettingsSerializer)
 25→
 26→    fun write(account: ValidatedSettings.Account): Pair<AccountDescription, AccountDescription> {
 27→        val editor = preferences.createStorageEditor()
 28→
 29→        val originalAccountName = account.name!!
 30→        val originalAccountUuid = account.uuid
 31→        val originalAccount = AccountDescription(originalAccountName, originalAccountUuid)
 32→
 33→        val accountUuid = getUniqueAccountUuid(originalAccountUuid)
 34→        val accountName = getUniqueAccountName(originalAccountName)
 35→        val writtenAccount = AccountDescription(accountName, accountUuid)
 36→
 37→        editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.$ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY", accountName)
 38→
 39→        // Convert account settings to the string representation used in preference storage
 40→        val stringSettings = AccountSettingsDescriptions.convert(account.settings)
 41→
 42→        for ((accountKey, value) in stringSettings) {
 43→            editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.$accountKey", value)
 44→        }
 45→
 46→        val newAccountNumber = preferences.generateAccountNumber().toString()
 47→        editor.putStringWithLogging("$accountUuid.accountNumber", newAccountNumber)
 48→
 49→        // When deleting an account and then restoring it using settings import, the same account UUID will be used.
 50→        // To avoid reusing a previously existing notification channel ID, we need to make sure to use a unique value
 51→        // for `messagesNotificationChannelVersion`.
 52→        val messageNotificationChannelVersion = clock.now().epochSeconds.toString()
 53→        editor.putStringWithLogging(
 54→            key = "$accountUuid.messagesNotificationChannelVersion",
 55→            value = messageNotificationChannelVersion,
 56→        )
 57→
 58→        serverSettingsWriter.writeServerSettings(
 59→            editor,
 60→            key = "$accountUuid.$INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY",
 61→            server = account.incoming,
 62→        )
 63→        serverSettingsWriter.writeServerSettings(
 64→            editor,
 65→            key = "$accountUuid.$OUTGOING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY",
 66→            server = account.outgoing,
 67→        )
 68→
 69→        writeIdentities(editor, accountUuid, account.identities)
 70→        writeFolders(editor, accountUuid, account.folders)
 71→
 72→        updateAccountUuids(editor, accountUuid)
 73→
 74→        if (!editor.commit()) {
 75→            error("Failed to commit account settings")
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        // Reload accounts so the new account can be picked up by Preferences.getAccount()
 79→        preferences.loadAccounts()
 80→
 81→        val appAccount = preferences.getAccount(accountUuid) ?: error("Failed to load account: $accountUuid")
 82→        localFoldersCreator.createSpecialLocalFolders(appAccount)
 83→
 84→        Core.setServicesEnabled(context)
 85→
 86→        return originalAccount to writtenAccount
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    private fun updateAccountUuids(editor: StorageEditor, accountUuid: String) {
 90→        val oldAccountUuids = preferences.storage.getString("accountUuids", "")
 91→            .split(',')
 92→            .dropLastWhile { it.isEmpty() }
 93→        val newAccountUuids = oldAccountUuids + accountUuid
 94→
 95→        val newAccountUuidString = newAccountUuids.joinToString(separator = ",")
 96→        editor.putStringWithLogging("accountUuids", newAccountUuidString)
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun writeIdentities(
100→        editor: StorageEditor,
101→        accountUuid: String,
102→        identities: List<ValidatedSettings.Identity>,
103→    ) {
104→        for ((index, identity) in identities.withIndex()) {
105→            identitySettingsWriter.write(editor, accountUuid, index, identity)
106→        }
107→    }
108→
109→    private fun writeFolders(editor: StorageEditor, accountUuid: String, folders: List<ValidatedSettings.Folder>) {
110→        for (folder in folders) {
111→            folderSettingsWriter.write(editor, accountUuid, folder)
112→        }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun getUniqueAccountUuid(accountUuid: String): String {
116→        val existingAccount = preferences.getAccount(accountUuid)
117→        return if (existingAccount != null) {
118→            // An account with this UUID already exists. So generate a new UUID.
119→            UUID.randomUUID().toString()
120→        } else {
121→            accountUuid
122→        }
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun getUniqueAccountName(accountName: String): String {
126→        val accounts = preferences.getAccounts()
127→        if (!isAccountNameUsed(accountName, accounts)) {
128→            return accountName
129→        }
130→
131→        // Account name is already in use. So generate a new one by appending " (x)", where x is the first
132→        // number >= 1 that results in an unused account name.
133→        for (i in 1..accounts.size) {
134→            val newAccountName = "$accountName ($i)"
135→            if (!isAccountNameUsed(newAccountName, accounts)) {
136→                return newAccountName
137→            }
138→        }
139→
140→        error("Unexpected exit")
141→    }
142→
143→    private fun isAccountNameUsed(name: String?, accounts: List<Account>): Boolean {
144→        return accounts.any { it.displayName == name }
145→    }
146→}

 10→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY
 11→import com.fsck.k9.AccountPreferenceSerializer.Companion.IDENTITY_NAME_KEY
 12→import com.fsck.k9.Preferences
 13→import com.fsck.k9.notification.NotificationSettingsUpdater
 14→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.ServerTypeConverter.fromServerSettingsType
 15→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.InvalidSettingValueException
 16→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.SettingsDescription

 19→import java.util.Calendar

 24→class SettingsExporter(
 25→    private val contentResolver: ContentResolver,
 26→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 27→    private val folderSettingsProvider: FolderSettingsProvider,
 28→    private val folderRepository: FolderRepository,
 29→    private val notificationSettingsUpdater: NotificationSettingsUpdater,
 30→) {
 31→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 32→    fun exportToUri(includeGlobals: Boolean, accountUuids: Set<String>, uri: Uri) {
 33→        try {
 34→            contentResolver.openOutputStream(uri, "wt")!!.use { outputStream ->
 35→                exportPreferences(outputStream, includeGlobals, accountUuids, includePasswords = false)
 36→            }
 37→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 38→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 43→    fun exportPreferences(
 44→        outputStream: OutputStream,
 45→        includeGlobals: Boolean,
 46→        accountUuids: Set<String>,
 47→        includePasswords: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 50→
 51→        try {
 52→            val serializer = Xml.newSerializer()
 53→            serializer.setOutput(outputStream, "UTF-8")
 54→
 55→            serializer.startDocument(null, true)
 56→
 57→            // Output with indentation
 58→            serializer.setFeature("http://xmlpull.org/v1/doc/features.html#indent-output", true)
 59→
 60→            serializer.startTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 61→            serializer.attribute(null, VERSION_ATTRIBUTE, Settings.VERSION.toString())
 62→            serializer.attribute(null, FILE_FORMAT_ATTRIBUTE, FILE_FORMAT_VERSION.toString())
 63→
 64→            Timber.i("Exporting preferences")
 65→
 66→            val storage = preferences.storage
 67→
 68→            val prefs: Map<String, Any> = storage.all.toSortedMap()
 69→            if (includeGlobals) {
 70→                serializer.startTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 71→                writeSettings(serializer, prefs)
 72→                serializer.endTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 76→            for (accountUuid in accountUuids) {
 77→                preferences.getAccount(accountUuid)?.let { account ->
 78→                    writeAccount(serializer, account, prefs, includePasswords)
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→            serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 82→
 83→            serializer.endTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 84→            serializer.endDocument()
 85→            serializer.flush()
 86→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 87→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e.localizedMessage, e)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids: Set<String>) {
 92→        try {
 93→            notificationSettingsUpdater.updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 94→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 95→            // An error here could mean we export notification settings that don't reflect the current configuration
 96→            // of the notification channels. But we prefer stale data over failing the export.
 97→            Timber.w(e, "Error while updating accounts with notification configuration from system")
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun writeSettings(serializer: XmlSerializer, prefs: Map<String, Any>) {
102→        for ((key, versions) in GeneralSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS) {
103→            val valueString = prefs[key] as String?
104→            val highestVersion = versions.lastKey()
105→            val setting = versions[highestVersion] ?: continue // Setting was removed
106→
107→            if (valueString != null) {
108→                try {
109→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting, valueString)
110→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
111→                    Timber.w(
112→                        "Global setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. This shouldn't happen!",
113→                        key,
114→                        valueString,
115→                    )
116→                }
117→            } else {
118→                Timber.d("Couldn't find key \"%s\" in preference storage. Using default value.", key)
119→                writeKeyAndDefaultValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting)
120→            }
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun writeAccount(
125→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
126→        account: Account,
127→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
128→        includePasswords: Boolean,
129→    ) {
130→        val identities = mutableSetOf<Int>()
131→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
132→
133→        serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
134→        serializer.attribute(null, UUID_ATTRIBUTE, accountUuid)
135→
136→        val name = prefs["$accountUuid.$ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY"] as String?
137→        if (name != null) {
138→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
139→            serializer.text(name)
140→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
141→        }
142→
143→        // Write incoming server settings
144→        val incoming = account.incomingServerSettings
145→        serializer.startTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
146→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(incoming.type))
147→
148→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, incoming.host)
149→        if (incoming.port != -1) {
150→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, incoming.port.toString())
151→        }
152→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, incoming.connectionSecurity.name)
153→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, incoming.authenticationType.name)
154→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, incoming.username)
155→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, incoming.clientCertificateAlias)
156→
157→        if (includePasswords && !incoming.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
158→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, incoming.password)
159→        }
160→
161→        var extras = incoming.extra
162→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
163→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
164→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
165→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
166→            }
167→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
168→        }
169→        serializer.endTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
170→
171→        // Write outgoing server settings
172→        val outgoing = account.outgoingServerSettings
173→        serializer.startTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
174→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(outgoing.type))
175→
176→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, outgoing.host)
177→        if (outgoing.port != -1) {
178→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, outgoing.port.toString())
179→        }
180→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, outgoing.connectionSecurity.name)
181→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, outgoing.authenticationType.name)
182→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, outgoing.username)
183→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, outgoing.clientCertificateAlias)
184→
185→        if (includePasswords && !outgoing.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
186→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, outgoing.password)
187→        }
188→
189→        extras = outgoing.extra
190→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
191→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
192→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
193→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
194→            }
195→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
196→        }
197→        serializer.endTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
198→
199→        // Write account settings
200→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
201→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
202→            val valueString = value.toString()
203→            val comps = key.split(".", limit = 2)
204→
205→            if (comps.size < 2) {
206→                // Skip global settings
207→                continue
208→            }
209→
210→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
211→            val keyPart = comps[1]
212→
213→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid) {
214→                // Setting doesn't belong to the account we're currently writing.
215→                continue
216→            }
217→
218→            val indexOfLastDot = keyPart.lastIndexOf(".")
219→            val hasThirdPart = indexOfLastDot != -1 && indexOfLastDot < keyPart.length - 1
220→            if (hasThirdPart) {
221→                val secondPart = keyPart.substring(0, indexOfLastDot)
222→                val thirdPart = keyPart.substring(indexOfLastDot + 1)
223→                if (secondPart == IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY) {
224→                    // This is an identity key. Save identity index for later...
225→                    thirdPart.toIntOrNull()?.let {
226→                        identities.add(it)
227→                    }
228→                    // ... but don't write it now.
229→                    continue
230→                }
231→
232→                if (FolderSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS.containsKey(thirdPart)) {
233→                    // This is a folder key. Ignore it.
234→                    continue
235→                }
236→            }
237→
238→            if (keyPart !in FOLDER_NAME_KEYS) {
239→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, keyPart, valueString, account)
240→            }
241→        }
242→
243→        writeFolderNameSettings(account, folderRepository, serializer)
244→
245→        serializer.endTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
246→
247→        if (identities.isNotEmpty()) {
248→            serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
249→
250→            // Sort identity indices (that's why we store them as Integers)
251→            val sortedIdentities = identities.sorted()
252→            for (identityIndex in sortedIdentities) {
253→                writeIdentity(serializer, accountUuid, identityIndex.toString(), prefs)
254→            }
255→            serializer.endTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
256→        }
257→
258→        val folders = folderSettingsProvider.getFolderSettings(account)
259→        if (folders.isNotEmpty()) {
260→            serializer.startTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
261→            for (folder in folders) {
262→                writeFolder(serializer, folder)
263→            }
264→            serializer.endTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
265→        }
266→
267→        serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
268→    }

270→    private fun writeAccountSettingIfValid(
271→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
272→        keyPart: String,
273→        valueString: String,
274→        account: Account,
275→    ) {
276→        val versionedSetting = AccountSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[keyPart]
277→        if (versionedSetting != null) {
278→            val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
279→
280→            val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
281→            if (setting != null) {
282→                // Only export account settings that can be found in AccountSettings.SETTINGS
283→                try {
284→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, keyPart, setting, valueString)
285→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
286→                    Timber.w(
287→                        "Account setting \"%s\" (%s) has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +
288→                            "This shouldn't happen!",
289→                        keyPart,
290→                        account,
291→                        valueString,
292→                    )
293→                }
294→            }
295→        }
296→    }

298→    private fun writeFolderNameSettings(
299→        account: Account,
300→        folderRepository: FolderRepository,
301→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
302→    ) {
303→        fun writeFolderNameSetting(
304→            key: String,
305→            folderId: Long?,
306→            importedFolderServerId: String?,
307→            writeEmptyValue: Boolean = false,
308→        ) {
309→            val folderServerId = folderId?.let {
310→                folderRepository.getFolderServerId(account, folderId)
311→            } ?: importedFolderServerId
312→
313→            if (folderServerId != null) {
314→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, folderServerId, account)
315→            } else if (writeEmptyValue) {
316→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, valueString = "", account)
317→            }
318→        }
319→
320→        writeFolderNameSetting(
321→            "autoExpandFolderName",
322→            account.autoExpandFolderId,
323→            account.importedAutoExpandFolder,
324→            writeEmptyValue = true,
325→        )
326→        writeFolderNameSetting("archiveFolderName", account.archiveFolderId, account.importedArchiveFolder)
327→        writeFolderNameSetting("draftsFolderName", account.draftsFolderId, account.importedDraftsFolder)
328→        writeFolderNameSetting("sentFolderName", account.sentFolderId, account.importedSentFolder)
329→        writeFolderNameSetting("spamFolderName", account.spamFolderId, account.importedSpamFolder)
330→        writeFolderNameSetting("trashFolderName", account.trashFolderId, account.importedTrashFolder)
331→    }

333→    private fun writeIdentity(
334→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
335→        accountUuid: String,
336→        identity: String,
337→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
338→    ) {
339→        serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
340→
341→        val prefix = "$accountUuid."
342→        val suffix = ".$identity"
343→
344→        // Write name belonging to the identity
345→        val name = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_NAME_KEY + suffix] as String?
346→        if (name != null) {
347→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
348→            serializer.text(name)
349→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
350→        }
351→
352→        // Write email address belonging to the identity
353→        val email = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY + suffix] as String?
354→        serializer.startTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
355→        serializer.text(email)
356→        serializer.endTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
357→
358→        // Write identity description
359→        val description = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY + suffix] as String?
360→        if (description != null) {
361→            serializer.startTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
362→            serializer.text(description)
363→            serializer.endTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
364→        }
365→
366→        // Write identity settings
367→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
368→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
369→            val valueString = value.toString()
370→
371→            val comps = key.split(".")
372→            if (comps.size < 3) {
373→                // Skip non-identity config entries
374→                continue
375→            }
376→
377→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
378→            val identityKey = comps[1]
379→            val identityIndex = comps[2]
380→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid || identityIndex != identity) {
381→                // Skip entries that belong to another identity
382→                continue
383→            }
384→
385→            val versionedSetting = IdentitySettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[identityKey]
386→            if (versionedSetting != null) {
387→                val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
388→                val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
389→                if (setting != null) {
390→                    // Only write settings that have an entry in IdentitySettings.SETTINGS
391→                    try {
392→                        writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, identityKey, setting, valueString)
393→                    } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
394→                        Timber.w(
395→                            "Identity setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +
396→                                "This shouldn't happen!",
397→                            identityKey,
398→                            valueString,
399→                        )
400→                    }
401→                }
402→            }
403→        }
404→        serializer.endTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
405→
406→        serializer.endTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
407→    }

409→    private fun writeFolder(serializer: XmlSerializer, folder: FolderSettings) {
410→        serializer.startTag(null, FOLDER_ELEMENT)
411→        serializer.attribute(null, NAME_ATTRIBUTE, folder.serverId)
412→
413→        // Write folder settings
414→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "integrate", folder.isIntegrate.toString())
415→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "inTopGroup", folder.isInTopGroup.toString())
416→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "syncEnabled", folder.isSyncEnabled.toString())
417→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "visible", folder.isVisible.toString())
418→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "notificationsEnabled", folder.isNotificationsEnabled.toString())
419→        writeFolderSetting(serializer, "pushEnabled", folder.isPushEnabled.toString())
420→
421→        serializer.endTag(null, FOLDER_ELEMENT)
422→    }

424→    private fun writeFolderSetting(serializer: XmlSerializer, key: String, value: String) {

426→        if (versionedSetting != null) {
427→            val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
428→            val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]

431→                try {

433→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {

436→                        key,

444→    private fun writeElement(serializer: XmlSerializer, elementName: String, value: String?) {
445→        if (value != null) {
446→            serializer.startTag(null, elementName)
447→            serializer.text(value)
448→            serializer.endTag(null, elementName)
449→        }

452→    private fun <T> writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(
453→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
454→        key: String,
455→        setting: SettingsDescription<T>,
456→        valueString: String,
457→    ) {
458→        val value = setting.fromString(valueString)
459→        val outputValue = setting.toPrettyString(value)
460→        writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, outputValue)
461→    }

463→    private fun <T> writeKeyAndDefaultValueFromSetting(
464→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
465→        key: String,
466→        setting: SettingsDescription<T>,
467→    ) {
468→        val value = setting.getDefaultValue()
469→        val outputValue = setting.toPrettyString(value)
470→        writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, outputValue)
471→    }

473→    private fun writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer: XmlSerializer, key: String, literalValue: String?) {
474→        serializer.startTag(null, VALUE_ELEMENT)
475→        serializer.attribute(null, KEY_ATTRIBUTE, key)
476→        if (literalValue != null) {
477→            serializer.text(literalValue)
478→        }
479→        serializer.endTag(null, VALUE_ELEMENT)
480→    }
